title: Isaocat Utilities
description: Just a bunch of ideas I had
body:
Just a bunch of ideas I had. Mod made with MCreator.

Optional Old Combat Addon: https://modrinth.com/mod/isaocats-old-combat-mod

JEI highly recommended: https://modrinth.com/mod/jei 

If you get the error "java.lang.reflect.InvocationTargetException: null", then you need to install the Curios API (1.19.4 Only)

Commands:
- "/sethome" sets your home point. This is a point you can teleport to with use of the magic mirror or the next command. (unlike the other commands, anyone can use this one)
- "/tphome" will teleport you to the home point described above.
- "/togglecreeperblossomcraters" will toggle whether or not creeper blossoms leave craters in the ground.
- "/spawntrader" will summon a merchant at your feet.
- "/MobSpawnsWithPotionEffectPercentChance #" will determine the chance that mobs spawn with potion effects. Accepts values 0-100.
- "/MobPotionEffectPotency #" will determine the level of potion effect that mobs can spawn with. Accepts values 0-100, but no more than 4 is recommended.
- "/MobPotionChancePotencyIncreasesYes" will enable the mob difficulty parameters increasing naturally. (It's an experimental option, so it is off by default)
- "/MobPotionChancePotencyIncreasesNo" will disable the mob difficulty parameters increasing naturally. (It's an experimental option, so it is off by default)

Lore:
The Hallow and The Corruption:
The hallow and corruption, both dimensions inspired by terraria, are dimensions that are the exact opposite. One is a peaceful paradise, (at least during the day), and one is a dark, musty dimension with lakes of poison. The hallow is located above the end, which is located above the overworld. The corruption is located below the nether, which is located above the overworld. You might have noticed the similarities between the corruption and nether. Both of them are large cave-like dimensions with lakes of hazardous liquids. The hallow is actually a giant island in the sky, it’s just too big to find the edge of. Both dimensions have rare, unique materials used in endgame crafting.

The Unicorns:
The unicorns once had a mechanical leader. They didn’t build it on their own, the builders did. The builders noticed that the zombie infection affected unicorns as well, and the unicorns were at risk of going extinct. So, they built a huge, mechanical unicorn to keep them safe. But it wasn’t enough. The mechanical unicorn soon became corrupt, and the zombie infection nearly wiped out the unicorns completely. If you wish to breed a few, pick some pink flowers from the hallow, the unicorns love them. If you ever end up killing one, their meat is the best… just don’t eat them all. They are an endangered species.

The Voidlings and Undead Chickens:
The Voidlings, main inhabitants of the corruption, are a mysterious civilization. They built cities, now in ruin, and each one carries a void diamond. The void diamonds are presumed to be the core of every voidling, and the cities… were built before… IT happened. A toxic gas soon filled the air of the dimension, and the Voidlings lost most of their intellectual power. They now wander the dimension, killing anything in their path. The chickens of the dimension were never the same as the ones in the overworld. Well, at least nobody ever saw one. It is thought that in the early days of the Minecraft world, chickens wandered into a rift, and soon adapted to life in the corruption. The chickens, confused by the toxic gas, acquired a taste for blood. The chickens attack everything they see.
